results.title: هاتفيًا.. ولي العهد يبحث تطورات الأوضاع بالمنطقة مع أمير الكويت
results.description:
أجرى صاحب السمو الملكي الأمير محمد بن سلمان بن عبدالعزيز آل سعود ولي العهد رئيس مجلس الوزراء، اتصالًا هاتفيًا اليوم الأربعاء، بأخيه صاحب السمو الشيخ مشعل الأحمد الجابر الصباح أمير دولة الكويت.

رفض المملكة للاعتداءات الإيرانية

وأعرب سمو ولي العهد، خلال الاتصال عن تضامن المملكة ووقوفها الكامل إلى جانب دولة الكويت الشقيقة وإدانتها الشديدة للاعتداءات الإيرانية الغاشمة والمتواصلة على أراضي دولة الكويت.
كما جرى خلال الاتصال بحث تطورات الأوضاع في المنطقة، والجهود المبذولة لتحقيق الأمن والاستقرار.

results.title: لجنة أصدقاء يحيى سكاف : للتضامن مع قضية الصحافي حسن عليق
results.description: اسلام تايمز (لبنان)_ دعت لجنة أصدقاء عميد الأسرى في السجون الصهيونية يحيى سكاف في بيان ، القضاء الى “عدم القبول باستخدامه كوسيلة ضغط من قبل قوى داخلية وخارجية على إعلاميين لبنانيين نتيجة مواقفهم كما يحصل اليوم مع الصحافي حسن عليق الذي يواجه حملةً يراد من خلالها إسكات صوته الحُر نتيجة مواقفه الوطنية المُشرفة ودفاعه الدائم عن القضايا المحقة لشعبنا و أُمتنا”.

results.description: رغم اشتباكه معه في نهائي المونديال.. غافي يدافع عن باريديس ويطالب بعدم معاقبته إرم نيوز إشادات واسعة بالأرجنتيني لوبيز لموقفه أثناء تتويج إسبانيا بالمونديال الشرق الأوسط مدرب الأرجنتين نادم على موقفه مع أولمو.. ويريد الاعتذار العربية اعتراف صادم من مساعد سكالوني بعد لكمة أولمو في نهائي المونديال Kooora رغم اعتدائهم عليه.. غافي يطلب عدم معاقبة لاعبي الأرجنتين CNN Arabic

results.title: OIC, Arab Parliament denounce Houthi threats, reaffirm support for Saudi Arabia
results.description: RIYADH — The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) and the Arab Parliament have condemned recent threats and accusations made by the Houthi group against Saudi Arabia, reaffirming their solidarity with the Kingdom and calling for the protection of maritime security in the Red Sea. In a statement issued on Tuesday, OIC Secretary-General Hissein Brahim Taha strongly condemned the Houthi group’s threats against Saudi Arabia, maritime navigation and the freedom of international trade. He called for the implementation of the relevant United Nations Security Council resolutions concerning Yemen and the protection of navigation rights and freedom of navigation in the Red Sea. He said the threats were part of a continuing pattern of attacks and baseless claims by the Houthis against the Kingdom, including recent missile attacks targeting southern Saudi Arabia and threats against maritime navigation. The Secretary-General stressed that the Houthi group’s military spokesperson’s accusations and threats were unacceptable and could not be justified in any form, warning that the continued threat to maritime navigation poses a danger to regional and international security. He reaffirmed the OIC’s full solidarity with Saudi Arabia and its support for all measures the Kingdom takes to safeguard its security and stability. He also highlighted Saudi Arabia’s efforts to contribute to peace, security and stability in Yemen, alongside the generous humanitarian and relief assistance, and development projects it has provided to ease the suffering of the Yemeni people. Similarily, the Arab Parliament — reiterating its full solidarity with the Kingdom — condemned statements made by the so-called military spokesperson for the Houthi terrorist militia accusing Saudi Arabia of imposing a blockade on the Yemeni people and a ban on maritime navigation. Speaker Mohammed bin Ahmed Al Yamahi rejected what he described as provocative and irresponsible remarks, saying they contradict Saudi Arabia’s continued efforts and ongoing support for the Yemeni people. He reaffirmed the Arab Parliament’s firm rejection of any offensive statements or threats targeting Saudi Arabia or undermining its sovereignty and national security, stressing that the Kingdom’s security is an integral part of Arab national security. Al Yamahi also called on the international community to take the necessary steps to implement the United Nations Security Council resolutions, particularly Resolution 2722, in protecting the freedom of navigation in the Red Sea and deter actions that threaten regional and international peace and security.
creator: saudi gazette

results.title: المفتي قبلان : لقاء عون – ترامب استبدل الـ 1701 برخصة طيران فارغة وترك السيادة اللبنانية ل”إسرائيل” الإرهابية
results.description: اسلام تايمز (لبنان)_ أصدر المفتي الجعفري الممتاز الشيخ أحمد قبلان، بيانا، لفت فيه الى “إنّ قمة الرئيس عون-ترامب” استبدلت الـ 1701 برخصة طيران فارغة وتركت السيادة اللبنانية لإسرائيل الإرهابية، والخطير في هذه القمة المأساة أنها كررت التزامها الكامل بأمن إسرائيل الإرهابية على حساب الأرض اللبنانية وسيادتها، و لذلك هي أشبه بمن يعالج مريض بحقنة سمّ، والقضية قضية بلد وسيادة وشراكة وطنية لا قضية من يقول للآخر أنت وسيم مقابل ابتلاع السيادة اللبنانية!”.
creator: islam times

results.title: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson wins defamation case brought by young actress
results.description: SYDNEY — Hollywood star Rebel Wilson has successfully defended claims she defamed a young actress who played the lead role in her directorial debut.A Sydney court on Wednesday dismissed the case brought by Charlotte MacInnes, who had accused the “Pitch Perfect” star of spreading lies about her to millions of followers on Instagram.MacInnes, who starred in Wilson's film The Deb, said the posts had made her the “subject of abuse, hatred and ridicule online” and affected her career prospects. MacInnes sued Wilson for allegedly harming her reputation in Instagram posts in 2024 and 2025.In the posts, Wilson accused MacInnes of retracting her complaint of sexual harassment against a film producer so she could further her career as an actor and singer. MacInnes denied ever having made a complaint.In court on Wednesday, Justice Elizabeth Raper dismissed the case, saying the imputation that MacInnes had changed her story about the incident was not defamatory. The judge ordered MacInnes to pay Wilson's costs.The claim and counterclaim are among serious allegations – including of smear campaigns and embezzlement – aired in multiple court cases now swirling around the production of the Australian musical comedy.Set in an outback Australian town, “The Deb” tells the story of two very different cousins – one from the city and one from the country – who unite ahead of a debutante ball. It was supposed to be a heartwarming tale of triumph, but its making has spawned a saga of its own.Wilson told “60 Minutes Australia” last year that the legal storm that engulfed the making of her first feature film was “like your worst nightmare,” and the issues had “come out of left field.”In an Instagram post after Wednesday’s ruling, Wilson thanked the judge and the Australian legal system for the “substantial win,” and said she was looking forward to moving on.One of the key claims related to an incident in September 2023, when MacInnes and producer Amanda Ghost shared a bath at a Sydney penthouse rented by the production company.In her ruling, Justice Raper said: “To say that the circumstances surrounding how they came to be in the bath are unusual would be an understatement.”MacInnes and Ghost said they were both in their swimsuits and entered the bath to warm up after Ghost had a physical reaction to swimming in the cold sea at the city’s Bondi Beach.Ghost said in her submission to the court that she was shivering uncontrollably and breaking out in welts that she hoped would disappear after warming up.MacInnes was also cold and the bath was big enough for both women, she said. Backing Ghost’s version of events, MacInnes said in her affidavit: “There was nothing sexual in any way.”However, Wilson claimed that MacInnes had told her that Ghost had asked her to take a bath and shower with her and that the experience made her “uncomfortable.”Wilson said she understood MacInnes to be reporting sexual harassment on set, and that as the director she was obliged to act on it.Justice Raper found that only one of the claims was “made out” – that MacInnes had changed her story about being uncomfortable, and that was not defamatory. The case was dismissed with MacInnes ordered to pay costs.Raper also found that MacInnes had failed to establish the posts had caused or were likely to serious harm.Wilson posted on Instagram moments after the decision, saying the "process has taught me a lot"."It has tested me, but it has also reinforced something I've always believed. I want to live my life standing up for what I believe is right, while continuing to create work that brings people joy."Wilson thanked her supporters and said she was "grateful this chapter has reached its conclusion". Earlier, lawyers for MacInnes said Wilson's allegations were "malicious concoctions" and the court heard claims the Hollywood star had ordered a smear website to target Ghost, which Wilson denied.Wilson's lawyer argued that MacInnes, who graduated from acting school in Western Australia in 2021, had not been harmed by the posts, pointing to a recent record deal that she had signed.
creator: agencies
